Bonus is considered interest and will be reported on IRS Form 1099-INT (or Form 1042-S, if applicable).   2Enrollment in Zelle® at a participating financial institution using an eligible U.S. checking or savings account is required to use the service. Chase Mobile app is available for select mobile devices. Enroll on the Chase Mobile app. Message and data rates may apply.   4Chase Online Bill Pay: Must enroll in Chase OnlineSM Banking and activate Online Bill Pay. Certain restrictions and limitations may apply.   5Account Alerts: There is no charge from Chase, but message and data rates may apply. Delivery of alerts may be delayed for various reasons, including service outages affecting your phone, wireless or internet provider; technology failures; and system capacity limitations. Any time you review your balance, keep in mind it may not reflect all transactions including recent debit card transactions or checks you have written.   Chase Mobile® app is available for select mobile devices. Message and data rates may apply. subject: Thank You + Quick Feedback from February Grassroots Tech Network
body: ￼ Chicago Grassroots Tech Community Meetup - February Feb 19, 5:30 PM CST · Midwest Coast Brewing Company ￼ Thank You + Quick Feedback from February Grassroots Tech Network  ￼ Ulysses David Hi everyone, Thank you again for joining us for our February Grassroots Tech Network meetup at Midwest Coast Brewing Company. We had over 210 registrations and more than 100 people show up in person. The room was full of strong conversations, new connections, and a great mix of engineers, founders, GTM leaders, students, and builders across Chicago. As we continue building this community, we would genuinely value your feedback. If you have a minute, please reply and share: 	•	What worked well  	•	What could be improved  	•	What you would like to see at future meetups  We have April and May already scheduled, and we are finalizing the location for March now. We are also looking for: 	•	Host locations for upcoming events  	•	Food and beverage sponsorship partners  	•	Volunteers who want to help shape the community  If you are interested in hosting, sponsoring, or getting more involved, just reply to this email and let us know. Our goal remains simple: consistent, high quality, in person connection across Chicago’s tech community. Thank you for being part of this and helping it grow. subject: Do your emails demand a reply? Join our webinar to find out
body: ￼  The art (& science) of writing emails  User Exclusive Live Webinar  This Thursday 10 EST | 3 GMT | 4 CET Have you ever written an email you thought would land you countless replies... but then nothing? If so, you're not alone. But you don't need to wonder why after joining our webinar this week. This is a webinar dedicated to sharing what we learned from 31 million outreach emails and why there are two parts of email writing that you need to think about - the technical and creative. From writing the perfect subject line, how many links to include, all the way to how many emails you need to send to a lead, we're sharing it and much more. If you want replies, you need to attend. Join us this Thursday In our live session, we're going to cover: 	•	The data behind what works: opens, replies, and leads 	•	  	•	A quick-win checklist before you hit send 	•	  	•	How to map buyer challenges to attention-grabbing content 	•	  	•	The way to make every email feel unique to your lead 	•	  	•	What NOT to write: The #1 complaint from decision-makers (and how to avoid it) 	•	  	•	Why sending from a Google account beats Microsoft 	•	    	•	Whether you should send well-designed or plain-text emails 	•	  	•	Your questions answered, LIVE 	•	   ￼   Can’t attend live? subject: We don't want (all of) your money
body: ￼ ￼ Just this once, let's talk about the money Hello. Two things we hear a lot... 	•	PostHog is really powerful.  	•	PostHog pricing is confusing. Sorry. Here's an explanation... 	•	You get a free allowance for every product each month, forever. 	•	You can add a payment method for more usage and priority support. OK. And if you do that then... 	•	You can set a billing limit of $0, if you still want it for free. 	•	You can always trust that we're the cheapest option though. OK. Now, let us never speak of payment methods ever again. It just feels gross, tbh. subject: Re: Shubham-Alpheva AI: Next Steps
body: Shubham:  Thanks for your email. 	1.	Did you check the iOS app or Android? 	2.	Could you please expand on this as I couldn't understand: There is no automatic use of the new AI chat window when I try to use pre-defined prompts like "What are my top expense categories from the last 30 days?" This chat got mixed with the old chat. 	3.	Generally, what helps is sharing screenshots with your findings in PDF or Word Doc; Is this something that you can share? Best,  Eva support@Utopia.cx | Linkedin ￼ Supporting Utopia Customers Scaling Alpheva AI: World's Smartest Financial Advisor    On Sat, Feb 21, 2026 at 9:11 PM Shubham Shakya <hi.shubham.shakya@gmail.com> wrote: Hey Eva,  It was a nice meeting with both Dinesh and Lord Mujal.  As discussed, here is my review of the Alpheva app.  I downloaded and used it for a while, and I really liked it. From a clean UI to making a connection with my banking app. Everything was very smooth and Impressive. I am giving a list of things I liked about and things that can be further improved.    Things I liked: 	•	I really liked its clean UI, nothing feels tightly packed, and information overloading when I opened it.  	•	The connected process was very smooth, not a hassle at all, just a few taps and verification. Impressive  	•	I also liked a feature to categorize my spending with left and right swipes.   	•	Information organization is done really well.   Things that can be improved: 	•	Overall app structure and UI were great, but I feel internal information management is an area where app can be improved. So my transaction structure is very complex, because I use Splitwise, so most of the things I buy will be split in transaction, there was no way I could manage that here, and I have to manage that in seperate app. I will never do it. So either you integrate Splitwise into your app, or you add a feature very similar to splitwise because I need to divide my actual transaction. My Solution is to use AI for information management. 	•	I was trying to access my last 1 year of transaction but I wasn't able to. I think there must be some bug or limitation. 	•	The AI chatbot feels very laggy. I mean, it was first processing the information and streaming as well, but it still feels very slow, as per my experience and understanding, it should not work like that. 	•	There is no automatic use of the new AI chat window when I try to use pre-defined prompts like "What are my top expense categories from the last 30 days?" This chat got mixed with the old chat.  Overall, I will say the App and idea feel great, but as an AI Engineer i feel there's a lot of scope for improvement in managing the internal data. You can also add a layer of personal intelligence with a small layer of memory. All different workflows seem very simple; you can use a more complex workflow to actually make your app smarter. In the real world, you divide the transaction many times with your friends, like rent, groceries, utility bills, etc. It is very difficult to actually manage those expenses; if we can't categorize those transactions, then the Alpheva AI system is not very smart.   I hope we can talk again and discuss how we can make it smarter.  Best Regards, Shubham Shakya   On Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 2:03 PM Utopia Alpheva AI <hello@utopia.cx> wrote: Hey, Shubham.  Just sent you an invite for 11:30 am CT.  Best,  Eva support@Utopia.cx | Linkedin ￼ Supporting Utopia Customers Scaling Alpheva AI: World's Smartest Financial Advisor    On Tue, Feb 17, 2026 at 9:29 PM Shubham Shakya <hi.shubham.shakya@gmail.com> wrote: Hi Eva,  I would like to schedule the call for this Thursday at 11:30 am. Could you please confirm the timezone for this time?  I will ensure I study the app workflow and industry before the interview.  Best regards, Shubham   On Tue, Feb 17, 2026, 8:49 PM Utopia Alpheva AI <hello@utopia.cx> wrote: Thanks, Shubham. How are you placed for a brief call with our CEO, Lord Munjal, this Thursday at 11 am or 11:30 am? Please do ensure that you study the app work flow and the industry before the interview.  Best,  Eva support@Utopia.cx | Linkedin ￼ Supporting Utopia Customers Scaling Alpheva AI: World's Smartest Financial Advisor    On Tue, Feb 17, 2026 at 6:53 PM Shubham Shakya <hi.shubham.shakya@gmail.com> wrote: Hey Eva,  Thanks for considering me. Here are answers to your questions: 	1.	Are you a U.S. citizen/Green Card Holder? If not, what's your Visa type & status I am not a US Citizen. I am on OPT.  	2.	If on OPT, then what type of OPT and what's the start date I am in my first year of OPT. It's already started from 16 Feb, 2026.  	3.	Updated copy of your resume. Attached to this email.  	4.	Current employment status. Working with a professor on a research project.  	5.	Current location Buffalo, NY(willing to relocate and do not need assistance)  	6.	If selected, how soon you can start. I can start as soon as 1 week.  Rating on skills: 	•	React Native (Front-End): Rating 6 and Months 3 (Freelancing) 	•	Go / Golang (Back-End): Rating 5 and Months 1.5 ( Freelancing) 	•	DevOps: Rating 8 and Months 12 (Deployed project to client server) 	•	LLM and Machine Learning: Rating 9 and Months 48  	•	Generative AI: Rating 9 and Months 36   Let me know if you need any other information. Looking forward to hearing from you. subject: Changelog: PostHog AI can now search the web
body: ￼ ￼ What's new on PostHog this month? Strap in for a fresh episode of the PostHog changelog, this time with a special guest appearance from PostHog AI. Want to catch up on old episodes? Check the playlist. Watch, like, and subscribe    ￼ New roles now available... We're hiring for a marketer to work on our data products, engineers for all of our product teams, product managers, and more. Apply now!   ￼ New in platform and betas... Feeling insecure? The platform team has added passkey support and notification options. Plus, tagging is now available for free!    ￼ New in observability... You found the error. You replayed the session. You sighed. Now check the logs, right inside PostHog, and see what actually happened.   ￼ New in AI and LLMs... The giant skeleton says that PostHog AI can now browse the web. What he doesn't know is that it can also help out with log investigations too. Snazzy.   ￼ New in data and analytics... There's a bunch of new data sources this month, including: GitHub, Attio, Mailchimp, and Redshift. Plus, heatmap visualizations for SQL queries.     ￼ New in workflows and exports... Prefer plain text emails? Workflows now supports that. Plus, it's easier than ever to update your flows with movable notes too.    ￼ New in flags and experiments... You can now bulk delete your flags if you want to tidy up a bit, or use semver operators if you want to target app versions. Plus, replay summaries!   ￼ New in error tracking and support... New exception autocapture controls are now available, plus there are new empty states for most products to help you get started.    Want more? Head to the full changelog Boring, legal bit: We're at 2261 Market Street #4008, CA 94114. Unsubscribe?  ￼ ￼ ￼ ￼

Omnilow is the algorithmic language that is as versatile as natural language for LLMs (large language models). Omnilow can express hyper-complex reasoning (such as comparing which stocks appear most undervalued) in a compact and unambiguous form that natural language or code would turn into pages of confusion. Omnilow is the next language upgrade for artificial intelligence. Computer Language → Natural Language → Omnilow Omnilow removes the ambiguity that slows an LLM’s backend pipelines and agent-to-agent exchanges all with hardly any learning curve. No LLM in the world is yet able to properly understand Omnilow and Omnilow is the only algorithmic language that is as versatile as natural language.         You're receiving this notification because you're looking for jobs on Wellfound  228 Park Ave S PMB 40533 · New York, NY 10003-1502  Click here to unsubscribe or manage email preferences    ￼

If there's a match, we will make an email introduction.         		Applied AI - Senior/ Staff Software Engineer, Lead  		Sprinter Health · United States · Remote  		$225-260K  ￼   ￼ You applied today  ￼ You should hear back in 1-2 weeks  ￼ Schedule your first interview  View my application  Browse more jobs         IN THE MEANTIME  	•	You can update your profile before your application is reviewed.  	•	Read up on on how to make your profile stand out even more  	•	Check out the other unique startup jobs on Wellfound          Sprinter Health  Reimagining last mile healthcare:    ACTIVELY HIRING B2B GROWTH STAGE TOP INVESTORS GROWING FAST     ￼   Location  Menlo Park, San Francisco Bay Area    Founders  Cameron Behar, Max Cohen    Company size  201-500    Total raised  $125M    Latest round  Series B in 2025    At Sprinter Health, we’re tackling one of healthcare’s biggest challenges: reaching patients who have fallen through the cracks of our healthcare system. With a boots-on-the-ground clinician network and the tech platform to solve this problem at scale, we bring critical preventive care to folks who are otherwise not getting it, whether due to disability, socioeconomics, transportation insecurity, or simply not getting around to it. Since launching in 2021, we're now live across 15+ states, covering over 60% of the US population and partnering with 6 of the 10 largest U.S. health plans. We've served >60k patients in-home from ages 3 to 103. And with 5x revenue growth in 2023 and 6x growth in 2024, things are only accelerating. Our team of technologists, clinicians, and operators bring decades of experience from Google, Meta, Amazon, Epic, Aetna, and Oak St. Health. It needs clarity into how models behave, where they fail, and whether they can be trusted. Tessel provides that clarity. We build the behavioral profile of AI systems and make it queryable by the stakeholders who rely on it — so submissions move faster, regulatory clearance decisions hold up, and deployment bets pay off. We're starting with FDA-regulated medical imaging, where the barrier to deployment is rarely performance; it's proof. The infrastructure to produce that evidence doesn't exist. subject: Only If You Are Using Deel - Update: Project Spectra
body: Hi everyone, If you are currently receiving payments through Deel, please review this announcement. If you are already using Stripe, you may disregard this message. subject: Thank you for your application to IMO Health!
body: Hi Shubham,   Thanks for your interest in IMO Health! We received your application and are excited about the potential for you to join the IMO Health team.   What happens now?   One of two things will happen next: 	•	Depending on the position, you may receive an invitation from recruiting@imohealth.com to complete a virtual video interview as a first step. This will be a few questions to help us get to know you better before scheduling you for a live interview with a member of our team. Or... 	•	A member of our recruiting team will reach out to you to schedule a phone interview.    What happens if you don’t hear from us right away? Don’t worry, we got your information and are reviewing your profile. You’ll hear from us either way so you know where you stand in our process.   Please note: As part of our interview process, IMO Health may use AI notetakers during phone and Zoom interviews to capture information in real time. These recordings and transcriptions are used for internal evaluation purposes only. By accepting an interview, you consent to the use of AI notetakers during the interview process.   Thanks!   The IMO Health Recruiting Team

subject: P0 Tasks are Auto-claim - Please Do NOT Click Into a Task Without Being Ready to Complete It: Project Spectra
body: Hi! Hope you had a great weekend! We are still seeing a lot of P0 tasks in_progress for longer than 12 hours - many of which are blank. We want to emphasize that P0 tasks are auto-claimed the moment you click into the task. Thus, please do NOT click into any P0 task when you are not ready to finish the task within 12 hours. Sharing the client announcement again below "also, i want to clarify on the time limit:  we would like you to claim the task when you can reasonably expect to finish a task in 12 hours.  for example, if you are about to go to bed and only have an hour to spare and not sure if you can finish this off the next morning, then do not claim a task at that point.  the ask is for when to claim tasks.  this does not mean we want you to pick and choose shorter tasks or give up on it if you find yourself not able to finish in time.  once you claim a task, please commit to it and finish it the best you can.   some tasks are more difficult than others, and sometimes things happen in your life, and we understand. in case you find yourself not able to finish in time, stick to the quality requirements and try to finish and submit as soon as you are able to."   Thank you for adhering to this setup!  Hayden ￼  View announcement  |  Update your notification settings
